preamble: The shift from the lateral approximant [l] to the voiceless velar fricative [x] is a rare form of velarization. It typically involves an intermediate stage where the lateral becomes 'dark' [ɫ], followed by the loss of the anterior (alveolar) closure, leaving only the velar friction.

examples.note: In some archaizing or eastern dialects, the historical dark L retains heavy velar friction, occasionally surfacing as a fricative.
